task: SC09 unconditional audio generation (spoken digits, 16 kHz, 1.024 s clips)
status: COMPLETE, with two corrections baked in. QUALITY headline ('matched_bs64'): at matched budget the wave operator beats softmax on FSD (~16 vs ~26) — this stands. EFFICIENCY: the earlier '~4x faster/cheaper' was vs a naive-softmax strawman and is RETRACTED — see 'efficiency_vs_flashattention': against FlashAttention, wave is slower+heavier at 1024 tokens, wins on speed only beyond ~8k tokens, and never wins on memory. Also corrected: the 07-22 'operator upgrade' gains were training budget, not the operator. Earlier blocks kept for provenance.
rationale: This is the only experiment in the project that probes a sequence length where wave-field's O(n log n) scaling has a real wall-clock advantage over O(n²) softmax. CIFAR at 64 tokens is too short for the asymptotic crossover (see benchmarks/attn_benchmark.png — wave field is ~7× faster than softmax at L=2048).
provenance: 2026-07-18 overnight run on an RTX 4090 24 GB, torch 2.4.1+cu124. First run with the AMP/no_grad self-conditioning gradient bug fixed (see wave_field/diffusion.py p_losses); the June 2026 numbers this table previously anticipated were produced under that bug and are invalid.
caveats: FAST preset: batch 256 / lr 4e-4 at 100 epochs = ~4× fewer optimizer steps than the June bs-64 runs — absolute FSDs are not comparable across nights, only across runs within this table.
Wave runs used the BASE wave operator only — the dynamic-filter / hyena-gating upgrades that flipped the CIFAR result were NOT enabled (--dynamic_filter/--gating were left at defaults).

Runs B and D hit CUDA OOM inside softmax attention: at batch 256 and L=1024 the attention matrix is ~4 GB per layer. D died in epoch 1 (no results); B survived training (its in-training eval numbers are reported, 1k samples vs 10k for A/C) but crashed before the final eval and checkpoint push.
additional_readouts: confident_accuracy: fraction of generated clips classified as a digit with confidence > 0.5
class_entropy: Shannon entropy of predicted-class histogram (uniform = log(10) ≈ 2.303 — catches mode collapse)
mel_distribution: side-by-side mean log-mel spectrogram, real vs generated vs difference

runs.name: standard + physics
runs.eval: epoch-100 in-training eval, 1k samples (final 10k eval + checkpoint lost: OOM after training completed)

runs.name: standard + AdaLN
runs.eval: none — CUDA OOM in epoch 1 (softmax attention matrix at batch 256, L=1024)

findings: softmax+physics decisively beats the base wave operator on audio at this budget (FSD 30.0 vs 43.6) and keeps far better class coverage (entropy 1.53 vs 0.83).

Both wave runs mode-collapse onto two digits ('two'/'six'); softmax does not. Collapse is therefore a property of the content-independent wave kernel, not the (now fixed) gradient bug — the same diagnosis that motivated the dynamic spectral filter on images.
The O(L²) memory wall is concrete: softmax OOM'd a 24 GB GPU at batch 256 / L=1024 while the wave runs fit easily — the memory half of the asymptotic argument, demonstrated in the target regime.

upgraded_operator_2x2.provenance: RTX 4090, torch 2.4.1+cu124, scripts/run_sc09_upgraded.sh. All runs: --attn wave --dynamic_filter --gating hyena, batch 64, 100 epochs, 10k-sample FSD. Conditional runs: --num_classes 10, CFG w=2.0.
upgraded_operator_2x2.question: Does the CIFAR-winning wave-operator upgrade also fix audio quality + the mode collapse, and does class-conditioning help on top?
findings: SUPERSEDED by the matched batch-64 run (see 'matched_bs64' below). These findings attributed the FSD drop and collapse fix to the operator upgrade, with a batch-256-vs-64 budget caveat. The matched run shows the caveat was the whole story: the improvement was training budget, not the operator.

Class-conditioning + CFG adds a further ~2x (to FSD ~8) and near-uniform class balance (entropy ~2.22). physics beats AdaLN only unconditionally (14.9 vs 19.0); with labels + CFG they converge, AdaLN marginally ahead (8.0 vs 8.5). This part still stands.

matched_bs64.provenance: RTX 4090, torch 2.4.1+cu124, scripts/run_sc09_matched.sh. All runs unconditional, batch 64, lr 1e-4, 100 epochs, 10k-sample FSD — matched to the upgraded-wave bs=64 runs so softmax / base-wave / upgraded-wave form one comparison with no budget confound.
matched_bs64.question: At matched budget and L=1024, does the wave operator beat softmax on audio quality, and how much of the 2026-07-18/22 gains were the operator vs training budget?

efficiency_benchmark_RETRACTED.status: RETRACTED — measured against naive materialized softmax (the O(L^2) strawman). Superseded by 'efficiency_vs_flashattention' below. The '3.9x faster / 4.2x less memory' claim does NOT hold against a real FlashAttention baseline.

findings: At matched budget the wave operator BEATS softmax on audio QUALITY: FSD ~16 (base wave) vs ~26 (softmax), with better class coverage. The earlier 'softmax wins on quality' was entirely the budget confound. THIS STANDS.

The dynamic-filter/hyena upgrade does NOT transfer from images to audio: at matched budget it helps physics slightly (16.9->14.9) but HURTS AdaLN (16.1->19.0). The 3-3.5x 'operator' gain claimed on 07-18/22 was training budget (bs64 vs bs256).
The mode-collapse fix was also budget, not the operator: base wave at bs64 already covers all 10 digits (entropy 1.36-1.82) vs 2 digits at bs256 (entropy 0.72-0.83).

EFFICIENCY CLAIM RETRACTED: the '4x cheaper' was vs naive softmax. See 'efficiency_vs_flashattention' — against FlashAttention, wave is slower AND heavier at 1024 tokens; the speed win only appears beyond ~8k tokens, and there is NO memory win at any length.

efficiency_vs_flashattention.provenance: RTX 4090, torch 2.4.1+cu124. scripts/benchmark_crossover.py (operator sweep) + scripts/benchmark_audio_models.py (full model). ALL softmax now uses F.scaled_dot_product_attention (FlashAttention/memory-efficient kernel), the correct modern baseline. Files: outputs/crossover/crossover.json, outputs/audio_bench/audio_bench.json.
efficiency_vs_flashattention.why: The earlier efficiency numbers compared wave against naive materialized softmax ((qk^T).softmax()@v), which builds the full L x L matrix. That is a strawman — nobody trains attention that way. Re-measured against FlashAttention the advantage largely disappears.

full_model_1024_tokens_bs64.note: Same benchmark as the retracted block, now with the fair SDPA softmax. Shows how much the strawman inflated softmax.
full_model_1024_tokens_bs64.verdict: At 1024 tokens FlashAttention is ~1.6x FASTER and ~1.4x LESS memory than wave. Strawman inflated softmax 4.4x (time) / 5.9x (memory).

operator_sweep_step_ms.setup: bs 8, dim 256, 8 heads, fwd+bwd; naive softmax OOMs past 4096

findings: SPEED: wave overtakes FlashAttention at ~8192 tokens. Below that flash is faster (4.5x at 1k); above it wave's O(L log L) pulls away from flash's O(L^2), reaching ~10x faster at 65k tokens. Genuine, honestly measured.

MEMORY: FlashAttention wins at EVERY length; wave uses ~1.4-1.5x MORE memory (FFT materializes complex spectra; flash tiles and materializes nothing). No wave memory advantage exists against a real baseline.
SC09 at 1024 tokens is BELOW the crossover: wave there buys better quality at higher compute + memory cost, not lower. The efficiency payoff belongs to genuinely long-context tasks (>8k tokens).

to_run_next: Test a genuinely long-context task (>8k tokens) where wave is faster than FlashAttention — 1024-token SC09 is below the crossover
Reduce wave's memory constant (in-place/fp16 FFT, chunking) to turn the speed-only win into speed+memory
Benchmark against Mamba/S4 and Hyena, not just softmax — the real sub-quadratic baselines
Understand why the dynamic-filter/hyena upgrade helps images but regresses audio AdaLN
